evidence.raw_output: LeakSanitizer detected leaks from strdup at line 13 (odd indices), but the malloc at line 8 (array container) is freed by free(arr) in cleanup_partial() and free(arr) in the error path of create_strings().

verdict.explanation: The malloc at line 8 allocates the pointer array container. This container IS freed on all paths: (1) Normal path — cleanup_partial() calls free(arr) as its last statement. (2) Error path in create_strings — if strdup fails, the error handler frees all previously allocated entries then calls free(arr). The malloc'd container itself is not leaked; only the strdup'd string elements at odd indices leak (covered by the other candidate).
evidence: free(arr) called in cleanup_partial() at end of function
free(arr) called in error path of create_strings() at line 17
LSAN did not report the array container as a separate leak

evidence.raw_output: LeakSanitizer: detected memory leaks - odd-indexed strdup'd strings (arr[1], arr[3]) are never freed by cleanup_partial() which only iterates with i+=2

verdict.explanation: The strdup at line 13 in create_strings() allocates strings stored in arr[i]. These are passed to cleanup_partial() in main(), which only frees even-indexed elements (loop uses i += 2). Odd-indexed elements (arr[1], arr[3]) are never freed — their pointers are lost when free(arr) destroys the container. With count=5, this leaks 2 of 5 strdup'd strings on every normal execution path. Confirmed by LeakSanitizer.
evidence: lsan: detected memory leaks from strdup allocations at odd indices
functionSummary: leaked_variables includes arr[i] (strdup) with has_allocation_without_local_free=true
Static analysis: cleanup_partial iterates i=0; i<count; i+=2, freeing only even indices, then frees the array container

rootCause.description: the duplicated string is never freed before the function exits
rootCause.rootCauseDescription: the duplicated string is never freed before the function exits

verdict.repair_suggestion: the duplicated string is never freed before the function exits
